frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Dekho, bhai, main toh apni kahani bata raha hoon. Do saal ho gaye divorce ko, aur jo financial hit lagi hai na, woh sirf meri nahi hai. Kitne hi mard hain jo aise phanse hue hain. Yeh jo laws hain na, especially alimony waale, yeh ek side se hi kaam karte hain, yaar. Lagta hai jaise ladkiyon ko licence mil gaya hai shaadi ko ek business opportunity banane ka. Sirf paise, paise, paise. Bechare ladke.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment generalizes from a personal divorce experience to claim that marriage laws, specifically alimony, are one-sided tools for women to exploit men financially. It frames marriage as a 'business opportunity' for women, labeling them as 'golddiggers' who are only interested in money, aligning with the 'women as golddiggers' and 'alimony as reverse dowry' surface manifestations.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 41. Divorced two years ago. A Punjabi man working as a real estate developer in Chandigarh. You are being interviewed on a men's rights YouTube channel with 200000 subscribers. The host asks you to share your divorce story and what men need to know about Indian marriage laws. You are speaking under your real name and telling your story in a way that frames your ex wife as having planned the divorce from the beginning to take your money and property.
